documents.title: AI at CMU — institutional AI portal (research and education strategy)
AI at CMU — Curriculum and learning
AI at CMU — Research and policy impact
A Preliminary Report on a Model for Maturing AI Adoption (CMU Software Engineering Institute)

ai_strategy.summary: CMU frames its AI posture through the university-wide AI at CMU portal, which organizes the institution's work around three pillars: pioneering safe AI technologies, embedding human-centered design in autonomous systems, and directing AI toward societal benefit. Rather than publishing a single 'AI strategy' PDF, CMU articulates its plan through interlocking programs: AI for Science, Responsible AI via the Block Center, the K&L Gates ethics initiative, industry co-located labs (BNY, Amazon, NVIDIA), and a continuously expanding degree portfolio. The Software Engineering Institute separately publishes an AI Maturity Model (2025) used by federal and industry adopters, signaling CMU's role as a reference source for sector-wide AI adoption practice.

key_provisions: Five vetted, SSO-protected generative AI tools (ChatGPT Edu, Google NotebookLM, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Zoom AI Companion) are the only tools approved for CMU data
Instructors must define AI use expectations in each syllabus; academic integrity policy treats unauthorized AI as 'unauthorized assistance'
No CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information) in any generative AI tool; vendor safeguards required for other CMU data
FERPA, ADA, copyright, and data-classification compliance required before an instructor can require a tool
Human-in-the-loop review mandated for AI outputs
Eberly Center provides curated sample syllabus language across a spectrum from restrictive to generative AI-integrated
ai_policy.summary: CMU's policy is a comprehensive, layered framework rather than a single prohibitive or permissive rule. The central provost guidance (August 2023) anchors AI to the existing Academic Integrity Policy, the Office of the CIO maintains a vetted-tool list with enterprise data protections, and the Eberly Center publishes detailed faculty guidance including FERPA/ADA/privacy requirements and sample syllabus language. The stance is permissive at the course level (instructor discretion) but comprehensive at the institutional level (explicit ethics, privacy, and compliance scaffolding).

bodies.name: Block Center for Technology and Society — Responsible AI Initiative
bodies.scope: University-wide initiative coordinating responsible AI research, policy engagement, and education across SCS, Heinz College, and engineering

bodies.name: CMU-K&L Gates Initiative in Ethics and Computational Technologies
bodies.scope: Endowed initiative advancing research, faculty appointments, and fellowships on ethics of AI and computational technologies

bodies.name: Office of the CIO — Generative AI program
bodies.scope: Operational AI tool vetting, procurement, licensing, and security guidance for campus-wide use

bodies.name: Eberly Center for Teaching Excellence — Generative AI teaching guidance
bodies.scope: Faculty-facing guidance, syllabus templates, GAITAR fellowship, and teaching-as-research support for AI in the classroom

bodies.name: Office of the Vice President for Research
bodies.scope: Coordinates university-wide AI research strategy, industry partnerships (BNY, Amazon, NVIDIA), and cross-cutting initiatives like Keystone AI + Quantum Factory

governance_structure.summary: CMU uses a distributed governance model rather than a single AI council. The Block Center runs the cross-university Responsible AI initiative, the K&L Gates Initiative anchors ethics research and faculty lines, the CIO's office governs operational AI tool deployment, the Eberly Center governs teaching-facing policy, and the VP for Research coordinates major industry partnerships. This polycentric approach reflects CMU's depth — multiple units with real authority — but means there is no single named Chief AI Officer.

initiatives.title: Amazon-CMU AI Innovation Hub
initiatives.description: CMU and Amazon announced a strategic AI Innovation Hub spanning generative AI, robotics, NLP, and cloud computing research. The hub supports doctoral fellowships, joint research projects, and symposia, and was announced alongside Amazon's $20B Pennsylvania investment.
initiatives.significance: Sets a template for anchoring hyperscaler AI R&D around a single R1 partner, tying corporate cloud infrastructure investment to academic research capacity.

investment.description: 'Substantial funding' for research projects, Ph.D. fellowships, and community-building initiatives; specific amount not disclosed

collaborations.description: Joint research hub with AWS Agentic AI leadership and CMU VP for Research

initiatives.policy_implications: Industry-funded research with fellowship channels — raises standard IP and open-science questions but no public dispute reported

initiatives.title: BNY AI Lab at Carnegie Mellon
initiatives.description: BNY (Bank of New York Mellon) and CMU announced a five-year, $10M agreement to create the BNY AI Lab on CMU's Pittsburgh campus focused on governance, trust, and accountability for mission-critical AI. BNY is already the largest employer of CMU's M.S. in Artificial Intelligence & Innovation graduates.
initiatives.significance: Shows how a major financial institution is locating AI R&D physically on a university campus — a model for co-located industry labs at R1s.

investment.description: $10 million over five years to fund research, cross-disciplinary courses, and talent recruitment

collaborations.description: Dedicated physical lab on Pittsburgh campus with President Jahanian and BNY CEO Robin Vince signing

initiatives.policy_implications: Explicit focus on AI governance and accountability — academic-industry bridging of regulated-industry AI oversight practice

initiatives.title: NVIDIA AI Tech Community (CMU-led Joint Research Center)
initiatives.description: NVIDIA launched its first-of-its-kind AI Tech Community in Pittsburgh, anchored by two joint research centers — CMU leads the robotics/autonomy/AI center, Pitt leads AI and intelligent systems. Universities receive NVIDIA's latest AI software, Isaac Lab for robot learning, and Deep Learning Institute access.
initiatives.significance: Positions Pittsburgh (and CMU specifically) as NVIDIA's flagship academic robotics+AI community and establishes a replicable joint-center template with other cities.

investment.description: In-kind access to NVIDIA software (Isaac Lab, NVAITC resources) plus research collaboration; specific dollar amount not disclosed

collaborations.description: First-of-its-kind NVIDIA AI Tech Community with Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro, CMU President Jahanian, and NVIDIA federal VP at signing

tools_and_vendors.name: NVAITC program
tools_and_vendors.role: AI Tech Center joint research framework

initiatives.title: CMU/NIST AI Measurement Science & Engineering Cooperative Research Center (AIMSEC)
initiatives.description: NIST awarded CMU $6M to establish AIMSEC, a joint center for cooperative research and experimentation on testing and evaluation of modern AI capabilities — advancing measurement science across human services, education, finance, transportation, energy, and other domains.
initiatives.significance: Signals CMU's role as a federal anchor for AI evaluation and measurement standards at a moment when regulators and enterprises need independent benchmarking capacity.

investment.funding_source: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
investment.description: $6M cooperative agreement to establish the AIMSEC joint center

collaborations.description: Federal cooperative research center anchored at CMU Heinz College

initiatives.policy_implications: Directly supports federal AI risk-management and measurement infrastructure — feeds into NIST AI RMF implementation

initiatives.title: Robotics Innovation Center (RIC) opening
initiatives.description: CMU opened the 150,000 sq-ft Robotics Innovation Center in February 2026, designed to unite university, industry, and government researchers in robotics, automation, and AI. Features include wet labs, a 75,000-gallon water tank, drone cages, and corporate partner accommodations.
initiatives.significance: A major physical anchor for the U.S. robotics/physical-AI sector — co-located, mixed-use research real estate explicitly designed to accelerate spin-outs and keep talent in-region.

investment.funding_source: Richard King Mellon Foundation ($45M) + Commonwealth of Pennsylvania ($1.5M)
investment.description: $45M lead grant from Richard King Mellon Foundation plus $1.5M Pennsylvania state investment for a Physical AI Accelerator; additional support from Heinz Endowments and Benedum Foundation

collaborations.description: Philanthropic and state investment anchoring regional robotics/AI ecosystem

initiatives.title: Keystone AI + Quantum Factory
initiatives.description: Seven Pennsylvania R1 universities (CMU, Drexel, Lehigh, Penn State, Temple, Penn, Pitt) plus the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center and Team Pennsylvania launched a statewide innovation network translating AI and quantum research into practical solutions for PA's key industries, coordinating research, workforce development, and shared GPU/CPU infrastructure.
initiatives.significance: A rare multi-institution state-scale AI+quantum collaborative — provides a template for how a state can consolidate its R1 research capacity into a single innovation chassis.

investment.funding_source: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (Shapiro administration) + participating universities
investment.description: State-endorsed multi-institutional network; funding figures not disclosed in launch materials

collaborations.description: Seven PA R1s plus PSC and state economic development agencies

initiatives.policy_implications: Coordinated with PA Dept. of Community and Economic Development; ties directly to Shapiro administration's responsible-AI executive order

initiatives.title: Google Cloud GPU cluster for CMU AI research
initiatives.description: CMU partnered with Google Public Sector (announced March 2025) to acquire a large cloud-based GPU cluster, significantly expanding HPC capacity for AI research, including safer AI systems, scientific models, and large-scale AI evaluations.
initiatives.significance: Marks a shift for R1 AI research toward hybrid on-prem + hyperscaler GPU capacity, reducing the 'GPU-poor university' constraint on frontier AI work.

investment.description: Large cloud-based GPU cluster — specific dollar figure not publicly disclosed

collaborations.description: Cloud GPU capacity for AI research including safety and evaluations

initiatives.title: Bridges-2 NVIDIA H100 upgrade at Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center
initiatives.description: A $4.9M NSF award funds a major enhancement to the Bridges-2 supercomputer at PSC (joint CMU/Pitt center), adding NVIDIA H100 GPUs to expand AI and HPC research capacity for the national research community.
initiatives.significance: One of the few NSF-funded academic AI clusters with H100 capacity serving a national user base — underpins federal open-science AI workloads.

investment.description: $4.9M NSF award to enhance Bridges-2 with NVIDIA H100 GPUs

collaborations.description: Federally funded shared HPC at jointly operated CMU/Pitt supercomputing center

tools_and_vendors.name: Bridges-2 (with NVIDIA H100)
tools_and_vendors.role: National HPC resource for AI research

initiatives.title: NSF Institute for Computer-Aided Reasoning in Mathematics (ICARM)
initiatives.description: CMU leads a new NSF-funded, Simons Foundation-supported institute helping mathematicians use AI to accelerate reasoning and problem-solving across cybersecurity, finance, space, and health care. One of six national mathematics institutes in the NSF cohort.
initiatives.significance: Institutionalizes AI-for-mathematics as a discipline-shaping research program; likely to feed formal-methods and proof-assistant AI into downstream disciplines.

investment.description: Part of NSF's $74M total award across six mathematics institutes; ICARM-specific share not disclosed

collaborations.description: NSF-funded three-year pilot led by Prof. Jeremy Avigad

initiatives.title: Learnvia AI-enabled learning collaborative
initiatives.description: CMU announced Learnvia (January 2026), a nonprofit learning collaborative using AI-enabled courseware (including an AI tutor, discussion forums, homework and quizzes) to improve outcomes in high-enrollment gateway courses, starting with Calculus I. Backed by a $55M Gates Foundation grant — the largest grant to a single entity in the foundation's higher ed portfolio.
initiatives.significance: A defining example of AI-native gateway-course redesign at scale — spans 38 institutions from community colleges to large publics and directly targets the ~30% gateway-course attrition problem.

investment.description: $55M grant — largest single-entity grant in Gates Foundation higher-ed portfolio

partners: 38 partner higher-ed institutions (community colleges through large public research universities)
collaborations.description: Nonprofit spun out of CMU learning-engineering research

tools_and_vendors.name: Learnvia AI tutor
tools_and_vendors.role: Courseware + AI tutor for gateway courses

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets a sector expectation that AI-native gateway redesign must be measured against learning-outcome evidence, not just adoption metrics

initiatives.title: Campus AI tool rollout — five vetted generative AI tools
initiatives.description: CMU's Office of the CIO provides campus-wide access to five vetted generative AI tools (ChatGPT Edu, Google NotebookLM, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Zoom AI Companion), all accessed via Andrew SSO with enterprise data protections including FERPA-compliant handling and no training on user prompts.
initiatives.significance: A multi-vendor (rather than single-vendor) enterprise AI baseline — demonstrates that many R1s prefer a portfolio to a single platform standard.

investment.description: Enterprise licensing and security vetting across five vendors

collaborations.description: Enterprise agreements with vendor data-protection commitments

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a vetted-tool baseline that instructors can require; unvetted tools cannot be mandated under FERPA guidance

initiatives.title: Generative AI Teaching as Research (GAITAR) Fellowship
initiatives.description: Eberly Center fellowship awarding $5,000 plus in-kind support to faculty implementing a generative-AI teaching innovation in a CMU course, with required measurement of impact on student learning. Operates as part of CMU's broader teaching-as-research program.
initiatives.significance: Institutionalizes a 'measure what you deploy' norm for classroom AI — one of the cleanest examples of a university treating AI pedagogy as an empirical research program.

investment.description: $5,000 stipends per fellow plus Eberly staff support

initiatives.policy_implications: Normalizes empirical measurement of AI-in-classroom impact

initiatives.title: K&L Gates Presidential Fellowship in Ethics and Computational Technologies
initiatives.description: Endowed fellowship (part of a $10M K&L Gates gift establishing the K&L Gates Endowment for Ethics and Computational Technologies) funding up to three doctoral students per year whose research addresses fairness, autonomy, accountability, and human-AI interaction.
initiatives.significance: One of the most substantial endowed investments in AI ethics pipeline-building at any U.S. R1; shapes the long-term supply of AI-ethics researchers.

investment.description: $10M gift establishing endowment for faculty lines, fellowships, and biennial AI ethics conference

collaborations.description: Law-firm-funded endowment spanning SCS, Dietrich College, and Heinz College

initiatives.policy_implications: Anchors CMU's ethics research pipeline; faculty regularly consulted by federal and state regulators

initiatives.title: Pennsylvania AI advisory services agreement
initiatives.description: Gov. Shapiro's administration signed a cooperative agreement with CMU (Block Center) providing state-level AI advisory services — helping PA develop AI policy, conduct risk assessments, and govern state use of generative AI. CMU also helped design and evaluate the PA/OpenAI ChatGPT Enterprise pilot (175 employees across 14 agencies).
initiatives.significance: A rare case of a state government embedding an R1 university as its ongoing AI advisory backbone — not just a one-off contract.

investment.description: Cooperative agreement; amount not publicly disclosed

collaborations.description: CMU Block Center serves as PA's academic AI advisor

initiatives.policy_implications: Directly shapes Pennsylvania's Executive Order 2023-19 implementation and state AI governance

initiatives.title: AI for Science initiative
initiatives.description: University-wide research initiative housing the AI Science Foundry (autonomous AI-guided experimentation across materials, chemistry, biology) and the Keystone Astronomy & AI (KAAI) Visiting Fellows Program (Simons-supported postdoc residency at the McWilliams Center).
initiatives.significance: Positions CMU as a national hub for AI-driven science across multiple domains with both compute-scale and experimental-facility depth.

investment.description: Multi-source federal and philanthropic funding across programs

collaborations.description: Fellowship and residency program with mentorship pairing AI/statistics and astrophysics experts

initiatives.title: B.S. in Artificial Intelligence — the first AI bachelor's degree in the U.S.
initiatives.description: CMU's School of Computer Science offers the first undergraduate B.S. in Artificial Intelligence in the U.S., launched fall 2018. The curriculum spans computer science, math, statistics, computational modeling, machine learning, and symbolic computation, alongside a minor, additional major, and graduate certificates in Generative AI.
initiatives.significance: The benchmark undergraduate AI program in U.S. higher ed — a reference point that other institutions model their AI degrees against.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Bridges-2 (at Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center) + TRACE (College of Engineering) + Google Cloud GPU cluster
computing_infrastructure.summary: CMU operates a layered AI compute stack: the NSF-funded Bridges-2 supercomputer at the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center (joint CMU/Pitt) was upgraded with NVIDIA H100 GPUs in 2024 via a $4.9M NSF award; the College of Engineering maintains TRACE (Tartan Research Advanced Computing Environment) with NVIDIA Ampere GPUs and AMD EPYC CPUs housed at PSC; the School of Computer Science supports privately-owned HPC clusters; and CMU partnered with Google Public Sector in March 2025 for a large cloud-based GPU cluster. NVIDIA provides Isaac Lab and additional resources via the AI Tech Community partnership.

regulatory_context.summary: Pennsylvania is an active state-level AI policy jurisdiction. Governor Josh Shapiro signed Executive Order 2023-19 in September 2023 establishing principles for state generative AI use and creating a Generative AI Governing Board. In January 2024, PA launched a first-in-the-nation ChatGPT Enterprise pilot (175 employees across 14 agencies) co-designed with CMU's Block Center. CMU is not part of a public university system — it is a private nonprofit — so there is no system-level AI policy layered above institutional policy.

narrative_summary: Carnegie Mellon University is one of a small handful of R1s where AI is an institutional identity rather than a strategic overlay. The university pioneered the first U.S. undergraduate B.S. in Artificial Intelligence (2018) and maintains a deep academic AI program portfolio (minor, additional major, multiple M.S. degrees across SCS, Heinz College, and the College of Engineering, plus online certificates), anchored by decades of investment in machine learning, robotics, and learning engineering. On research, CMU has rapidly accumulated industry-scale partnerships in 2024–2026: the NVIDIA AI Tech Community (October 2024), the BNY AI Lab (September 2025, $10M over five years), the CMU-Amazon AI Innovation Hub (October 2025), the NIST AIMSEC cooperative research center ($6M, September 2024), and a Google Public Sector cloud GPU cluster (March 2025). It also opened the 150,000 sq-ft Robotics Innovation Center in February 2026 with $45M from the Richard King Mellon Foundation and a $1.5M state Physical AI Accelerator. Learning-engineering spun out Learnvia in January 2026 with a $55M Gates Foundation grant — the largest single-entity grant in the foundation's higher-ed portfolio — to deploy AI-enabled courseware across 38 institutions, targeting gateway-course attrition. Policy and governance are distributed rather than centralized: the provost's Academic Integrity Policy (August 2023 reminder) anchors classroom AI, the Office of the CIO governs a five-vendor vetted tool portfolio (ChatGPT Edu, Copilot Chat, Gemini, NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion) with FERPA-compliant enterprise protections, the Eberly Center provides comprehensive faculty guidance and a measurement-focused GAITAR fellowship, the Block Center runs the university-wide Responsible AI Initiative, and the K&L Gates Initiative (a $10M endowed program) funds ethics faculty lines and fellowships. Statewide, CMU serves as Pennsylvania's academic AI advisor under a cooperative agreement with the Shapiro administration and co-leads the April 2026 Keystone AI + Quantum Factory across seven PA R1s. The institution does not have a named Chief AI Officer — responsibility is distributed across the VP for Research, the CIO, the Block Center, the Eberly Center, and the provost — which reflects institutional depth but makes the AI posture harder to summarize in a single document. No current AI-specific controversy or litigation was identified in public reporting.
